syntax.command: python3 -m py_compile for all Python files

typecheck.command: N/A (no mypy installed, Python syntax checks pass)

lint.command: N/A (no flake8 installed, Python syntax checks pass)

nonBlockingIssues.description: AWQ kernel only applied to down_proj, not gate+up projections. The gemv_int4_dual_fused kernel (used for gate+up) still performs zero-point subtraction even though zeros=0 in AWQ. This is functionally correct but misses ~2/3 of the potential AWQ speedup benefit.

Validation assertion IDs in docstring and code (VAL-AWQ-005/006/007) don't match validation contract (VAL-AWQ-003/004). VAL-AWQ-003 is throughput (>= 42 tok/s), VAL-AWQ-004 is correctness (cosine sim >= 0.99). The code functionality is correct but assertion IDs should match contract for traceability.

appliedUpdates.description: AWQ integration documentation already present in architecture.md. No new entries needed.

rejectedObservations.reason: The benchmark is a one-time validation test, not a recurring service. The existing test_tp4 command pattern in services.yaml is sufficient for running the benchmark with the appropriate environment setup.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document API inconsistency between InferenceEngine.build_decode_launch_cache() and TPInferenceEngine.build_dispatch_cache()
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in architecture.md. The inconsistency is intentional - single-GPU uses 'launch_cache' while TP uses 'dispatch_cache' reflecting different execution models.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Kernel selection follows priority order (AWQ > v5 > v3). This pattern is consistently implemented in engine.py and documented in code comments.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/engine.py:2771-2779 shows priority: if _awq_mode and _gemv_int4_v5_awq → AWQ kernel, elif _gemv_int4_v5 → v5, else fallback

sharedStateObservations.observation: AWQ vs GPTQ quantization difference: AWQ uses w = q * scale (no zero-point) while GPTQ uses w = (q - zero) * scale. This eliminates 8 v_sub_f32 instructions per uint32 word.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: notes/AWQ_INTEGRATION.md and src/kernels/gemv_int4_v5_awq.hip:15-22 document the compute savings

sharedStateObservations.observation: set_awq_mode() should be called BEFORE build_dispatch_cache() for optimal performance. Calling after triggers automatic cache rebuild with performance warning.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/tp_engine.py:2833-2837 shows automatic cache rebuild logic

sharedStateObservations.observation: Correctness tests comparing TP=4 vs single-GPU show declining cosine similarity over steps due to numerical precision differences in allreduce reduction order. This is expected behavior for TP inference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/test_awq_e2e.py uses step-to-step coherence approach instead
